revision.text:
{{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} is the name of a [[country]] or [[location]] that appears in the [[book of Sui]] as being in the region of [[Wa (Japan)|Wa]].
== Outline == The {{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} appears in the [[book of Sui]] in "{{Lang|zh-tw|Ring 46}} [[Japanese missions to Sui China]] brought by Japanese envoys to the Sui dynasty (俀国) sent by king {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft}} to [[Emperor Yang of Sui]]. Mainly in the western part of the [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku region]].<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>
{{Quote frame|In the next year, the king sent Pei Qing, a royal official, to the night country, to Baekje, to Jukishima, south to Danla, through Dusma, in the middle of the sea, and east to a country, and then to Jukish, and then east to the kingdom of Qin, whose people were the same as Hwaha, and who thought that Yizhou could not be understood.|{{Lang|zh-tw|Sui Shu (Sui Book), Legend 46, Dong Yi, Tonight's Kingdom}}<ref>[http://www001.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai/zuisho/frame/zuisho_frame.htm 古代史獺祭 隋書 倭国伝隋書 卷八十一 列傳第四十六 東夷傳 倭國]</ref>}}{{Quote frame|In the next year, the emperor sent Pei Shiqing to the country of Tonight, and he arrived at the island of Bamboo, and looked south at the country of Tantra, and passed through the country of Dusma, which was in the middle of the sea, and then east to the country of Yi, and then to the country of Zhus, and then east to the country of Qin. |{{Lang|zh-tw|Northern History Vol. 94, Liezhuan No. 82}}<ref>[http://www.seisaku.bz/rekidai_waden/101_hokushi.html 北史卷九十四／列傳第八十二]</ref>}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also ==
* [[Toyo Province]] * [[Hata clan]] * [[Ikikoku]] * [[Chikushikoku]] {{Yamatai}} ]]
{{draft categories| [[Category:Former countries in Japanese history]] [[Category:History of the Kyushu region]] [[Category:Wajinden]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:States of the Wajinden]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia 4]] [[Category:11 4]] [[Category:mm]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Shin'okoku]] [[Category:bump 1-26]] [[Category:kk]] [[Category:second attempt at refreshing on January 27 2024]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Pages and Categories from Wikipedia]] [[Category:500 shortest pages as of 03-29-2025]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:pages which might possibly be incomplete but are most likely complete]] [[Category:I think I cleared all the incomplete or blank pages earlier]] }} [[en:Shin'okoku]] [[sn:Shin'okoku]]

== Outline == The {{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} appears in the [[book of Sui]] in "{{Lang|zh-tw|Ring 46}} [[Japanese missions to Sui China]] brought by Japanese envoys to the Sui dynasty (俀国) sent by king {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=draft}} to [[Emperor Yang of Sui]]. Mainly in the western part of the [[Chūgoku region|Chugoku region]].<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>
{{Quote frame|In the next year, the king sent Pei Qing, a royal official, to the night country, to Baekje, to Jukishima, south to Danla, through Dusma, in the middle of the sea, and east to a country, and then to Jukish, and then east to the kingdom of Qin, whose people were the same as Hwaha, and who thought that Yizhou could not be understood.|{{Lang|zh-tw|Sui Shu (Sui Book), Legend 46, Dong Yi, Tonight's Kingdom}}<ref>[http://www001.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai/zuisho/frame/zuisho_frame.htm 古代史獺祭 隋書 倭国伝隋書 卷八十一 列傳第四十六 東夷傳 倭國]</ref>}}{{Quote frame|In the next year, the emperor sent Pei Shiqing to the country of Tonight, and he arrived at the island of Bamboo, and looked south at the country of Tantra, and passed through the country of Dusma, which was in the middle of the sea, and then east to the country of Yi, and then to the country of Zhus, and then east to the country of Qin. |{{Lang|zh-tw|Northern History Vol. 94, Liezhuan No. 82}}<ref>[http://www.seisaku.bz/rekidai_waden/101_hokushi.html 北史卷九十四／列傳第八十二]</ref>}}

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|qq=draft|" to "|"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shin'okoku]][[ja:秦王国]][[zh:秦王国]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q6398782]] Wikidata name: Shin'ōkoku [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:21, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} is the name of a [[:en:Country|Country]] or [[:en:location|location]] that appears in the [[:en:Book of Sui|Book of Sui]] as being in the region of [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]].
== Outline == The {{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} appears in the [[:en:Book of Sui|Book of Sui]] in "{{Lang|zh-tw|Ring 46}} [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Japanese missions to Sui China]] brought by Japanese envoys to the Sui dynasty (俀国) sent by king {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} to [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Emperor Yang of Sui]]. Mainly in the western part of the [[:en:Chūgoku region|Chugoku region]].<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[:en:厳島|厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[:en:秦氏|秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>
{{Quote frame|In the next year, the king sent Pei Qing, a royal official, to the night country, to Baekje, to Jukishima, south to Danla, through Dusma, in the middle of the sea, and east to a country, and then to Jukish, and then east to the kingdom of Qin, whose people were the same as Hwaha, and who thought that Yizhou could not be understood.|{{Lang|zh-tw|Sui Shu (Sui Book), Legend 46, Dong Yi, Tonight's Kingdom}}<ref>[http://www001.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai/zuisho/frame/zuisho_frame.htm 古代史獺祭 隋書 倭国伝隋書 卷八十一 列傳第四十六 東夷傳 倭國]</ref>}}{{Quote frame|In the next year, the emperor sent Pei Shiqing to the country of Tonight, and he arrived at the island of Bamboo, and looked south at the country of Tantra, and passed through the country of Dusma, which was in the middle of the sea, and then east to the country of Yi, and then to the country of Zhus, and then east to the country of Qin. |{{Lang|zh-tw|Northern History Vol. 94, Liezhuan No. 82}}<ref>[http://www.seisaku.bz/rekidai_waden/101_hokushi.html 北史卷九十四／列傳第八十二]</ref>}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also ==

== Outline == The {{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} appears in the [[:en:Book of Sui|Book of Sui]] in "{{Lang|zh-tw|Ring 46}} [[:en:Japanese missions to Sui China|Japanese missions to Sui China]] brought by Japanese envoys to the Sui dynasty (俀国) sent by king {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} to [[:en:Emperor Yang of Sui|Emperor Yang of Sui]]. Mainly in the western part of the [[:en:Chūgoku region|Chugoku region]].<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[:en:厳島|厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[:en:秦氏|秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>
{{Quote frame|In the next year, the king sent Pei Qing, a royal official, to the night country, to Baekje, to Jukishima, south to Danla, through Dusma, in the middle of the sea, and east to a country, and then to Jukish, and then east to the kingdom of Qin, whose people were the same as Hwaha, and who thought that Yizhou could not be understood.|{{Lang|zh-tw|Sui Shu (Sui Book), Legend 46, Dong Yi, Tonight's Kingdom}}<ref>[http://www001.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai/zuisho/frame/zuisho_frame.htm 古代史獺祭 隋書 倭国伝隋書 卷八十一 列傳第四十六 東夷傳 倭國]</ref>}}{{Quote frame|In the next year, the emperor sent Pei Shiqing to the country of Tonight, and he arrived at the island of Bamboo, and looked south at the country of Tantra, and passed through the country of Dusma, which was in the middle of the sea, and then east to the country of Yi, and then to the country of Zhus, and then east to the country of Qin. |{{Lang|zh-tw|Northern History Vol. 94, Liezhuan No. 82}}<ref>[http://www.seisaku.bz/rekidai_waden/101_hokushi.html 北史卷九十四／列傳第八十二]</ref>}}

Mainly in the western part of the [[:en:Chūgoku region|Chugoku region]].<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[:en:厳島|厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[:en:秦氏|秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>
{{Quote frame|In the next year, the king sent Pei Qing, a royal official, to the night country, to Baekje, to Jukishima, south to Danla, through Dusma, in the middle of the sea, and east to a country, and then to Jukish, and then east to the kingdom of Qin, whose people were the same as Hwaha, and who thought that Yizhou could not be understood.|{{Lang|zh-tw|Sui Shu (Sui Book), Legend 46, Dong Yi, Tonight's Kingdom}}<ref>[http://www001.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai/zuisho/frame/zuisho_frame.htm 古代史獺祭 隋書 倭国伝隋書 卷八十一 列傳第四十六 東夷傳 倭國]</ref>}}{{Quote frame|In the next year, the emperor sent Pei Shiqing to the country of Tonight, and he arrived at the island of Bamboo, and looked south at the country of Tantra, and passed through the country of Dusma, which was in the middle of the sea, and then east to the country of Yi, and then to the country of Zhus, and then east to the country of Qin. |{{Lang|zh-tw|Northern History Vol. 94, Liezhuan No. 82}}<ref>[http://www.seisaku.bz/rekidai_waden/101_hokushi.html 北史卷九十四／列傳第八十二]</ref>}}

Mainly in the western part of the [[:en:Chūgoku region|Chugoku region]].<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[:en:厳島|厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[:en:秦氏|秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>
{{Quote frame|In the next year, the king sent Pei Qing, a royal official, to the night country, to Baekje, to Jukishima, south to Danla, through Dusma, in the middle of the sea, and east to a country, and then to Jukish, and then east to the kingdom of Qin, whose people were the same as Hwaha, and who thought that Yizhou could not be understood.|{{Lang|zh-tw|Sui Shu (Sui Book), Legend 46, Dong Yi, Tonight's Kingdom}}<ref>[http://www001.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai/zuisho/frame/zuisho_frame.htm 古代史獺祭 隋書 倭国伝隋書 卷八十一 列傳第四十六 東夷傳 倭國]</ref>}}{{Quote frame|In the next year, the emperor sent Pei Shiqing to the country of Tonight, and he arrived at the island of Bamboo, and looked south at the country of Tantra, and passed through the country of Dusma, which was in the middle of the sea, and then east to the country of Yi, and then to the country of Zhus, and then east to the country of Qin. |{{Lang|zh-tw|Northern History Vol. 94, Liezhuan No. 82}}<ref>[http://www.seisaku.bz/rekidai_waden/101_hokushi.html 北史卷九十四／列傳第八十二]</ref>}}

== Outline == The {{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} appears in the {{ill|Book of Sui|en|Book of Sui|WD=Q1156924|lt=Book of Sui}} in "{{Lang|zh-tw|Ring 46}} {{ill|Japanese missions to Sui China|en|Japanese missions to Sui China|WD=Q15401774|lt=Japanese missions to Sui China}} brought by Japanese envoys to the Sui dynasty (俀国) sent by king {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} to {{ill|Emperor Yang of Sui|en|Emperor Yang of Sui|WD=Q7419|lt=Emperor Yang of Sui}}. Mainly in the western part of the {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku region}}.<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[:en:厳島|厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[:en:秦氏|秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>
{{Quote frame|In the next year, the king sent Pei Qing, a royal official, to the night country, to Baekje, to Jukishima, south to Danla, through Dusma, in the middle of the sea, and east to a country, and then to Jukish, and then east to the kingdom of Qin, whose people were the same as Hwaha, and who thought that Yizhou could not be understood.|{{Lang|zh-tw|Sui Shu (Sui Book), Legend 46, Dong Yi, Tonight's Kingdom}}<ref>[http://www001.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai/zuisho/frame/zuisho_frame.htm 古代史獺祭 隋書 倭国伝隋書 卷八十一 列傳第四十六 東夷傳 倭國]</ref>}}{{Quote frame|In the next year, the emperor sent Pei Shiqing to the country of Tonight, and he arrived at the island of Bamboo, and looked south at the country of Tantra, and passed through the country of Dusma, which was in the middle of the sea, and then east to the country of Yi, and then to the country of Zhus, and then east to the country of Qin. |{{Lang|zh-tw|Northern History Vol. 94, Liezhuan No. 82}}<ref>[http://www.seisaku.bz/rekidai_waden/101_hokushi.html 北史卷九十四／列傳第八十二]</ref>}}
== References == {{Reflist}}
== See also ==
* {{ill|Toyo Province|en|Toyo Province|WD=Q1205043|lt=Toyo Province}} * {{ill|Hata clan|en|Hata clan|WD=Q987290|lt=Hata clan}} * [[Iki Island|Ikikoku]] * {{ill|Chikushikoku|en|Chikushikoku|WD=Q97200371|lt=Chikushikoku}} {{Yamatai}}
[[en:Shin'okoku]] <!--enwiki derived wikidata interwikis--> [[en:Shin'okoku]] [[ja:秦王国]] [[zh:秦王国]] {{wikidata link|Q6398782}} [[Category:Former countries in Japanese history]] [[Category:History of the Kyushu region]] [[Category:Pages with unreviewed translations]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Shin'okoku]] [[Category:States of the Wajinden]] [[Category:Wajinden]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Pages without translation templates]]

Mainly in the western part of the {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku region}}.<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[:en:厳島|厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[:en:秦氏|秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shin'okoku]][[ja:秦王国]][[zh:秦王国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shin'okoku]][[ja:秦王国]][[zh:秦王国]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Shin'okoku]][[ja:秦王国]][[zh:秦王国]]
{{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} is the name of a {{ill|Country|en|Country|WD=Q6256|lt=Country|1=Country}} or {{ill|location|en|location|WD=Q2221906|lt=location|1=Location}} that appears in the {{ill|Book of Sui|en|Book of Sui|WD=Q1156924|lt=Book of Sui|1=Book of Sui}} as being in the region of [[:en:Wa (Japan)|Wa]].
== Outline == The {{nihongo|Shin'okoku|秦王国}} appears in the {{ill|Book of Sui|en|Book of Sui|WD=Q1156924|lt=Book of Sui|1=Book of Sui}} in "{{Lang|zh-tw|Ring 46}} {{ill|Japanese missions to Sui China|en|Japanese missions to Sui China|WD=Q15401774|lt=Japanese missions to Sui China|1=Japanese missions to Sui China}} brought by Japanese envoys to the Sui dynasty (俀国) sent by king {{ill|Tarishihiko|ja|多利思比孤|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tarishihiko|qq=}} to {{ill|Emperor Yang of Sui|en|Emperor Yang of Sui|WD=Q7419|lt=Emperor Yang of Sui|1=Emperor Yang of Sui}}. Mainly in the western part of the {{ill|Chūgoku region|en|Chūgoku region|WD=Q127864|lt=Chugoku region|1=Chūgoku region}}.<ref>不詳であるが、松下見林は[[:en:厳島|厳島]]とし、山田安栄は[[周防]]の音を秦王に写したとする。山陽道西部にあった[[:en:秦氏|秦氏]]の居住地とも関係あるまいか。『新訂 魏志倭人伝・後漢書倭伝・宋書倭国伝・隋書倭国伝 中国正史日本伝(1) [[石原道博]]編訳 岩波文庫』P72</ref>
{{Quote frame|In the next year, the king sent Pei Qing, a royal official, to the night country, to Baekje, to Jukishima, south to Danla, through Dusma, in the middle of the sea, and east to a country, and then to Jukish, and then east to the kingdom of Qin, whose people were the same as Hwaha, and who thought that Yizhou could not be understood.|{{Lang|zh-tw|Sui Shu (Sui Book), Legend 46, Dong Yi, Tonight's Kingdom}}<ref>[http://www001.upp.so-net.ne.jp/dassai/zuisho/frame/zuisho_frame.htm 古代史獺祭 隋書 倭国伝隋書 卷八十一 列傳第四十六 東夷傳 倭國]</ref>}}{{Quote frame|In the next year, the emperor sent Pei Shiqing to the country of Tonight, and he arrived at the island of Bamboo, and looked south at the country of Tantra, and passed through the country of Dusma, which was in the middle of the sea, and then east to the country of Yi, and then to the country of Zhus, and then east to the country of Qin. |{{Lang|zh-tw|Northern History Vol. 94, Liezhuan No. 82}}<ref>[http://www.seisaku.bz/rekidai_waden/101_hokushi.html 北史卷九十四／列傳第八十二]</ref>}}
